title: [v3.0.1-rhel] Bump to runc v1.0.0-rc94
body:
Bump runc to v1.0.0-rc94 to avoid an exec issue as noted in
https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1983582

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.0.1-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mheon any concerns with this bump?  This is for RHEL 8.4GA

> unknown capability "CAP_PERFMON"

Gosh, how did we address that?  Didn't that a require a crun bump _and_ some changes in c/common? Cc: @rhatdan @giuseppe
I believe this requires a new libcap.
What exactly is bringing in this capability?

I have no access to that BZ so I can't give details, but I strongly question the need to vendor runc into Podman - we don't use much of the runc code and I really doubt this will fix whatever is happening.
I agree, this seems like a new runc might be required?
The bug in question is about issue fixed by https://github.com/containerd/console/pull/51, so no runc vendor bump is required.
Thanks for the looksee @kolyshkin , I'll close this then.
